name: Frondle
concept: A round, bashful kelp sprout that hides its face behind one big paddle-shaped leaf — except the leaf is translucent, so its enormous eyes and glowing coral blush shine right through, betraying it every time.
imagePrompt:
A chubby, lovable little sea-kelp creature named Frondle, standing full-body and facing the viewer, drawn in a bold flat-vector style: crisp geometric shapes, clean flat colour fills, no gradients except one soft translucent overlay, a confident uniform dark-teal die-cut outline of even weight all the way around.

SILHOUETTE AND PROPORTIONS: One single connected shape, wide and stable. The body is a fat rounded teardrop — a plump sea-green bulb, widest at the hips, about two heads tall, with no visible neck. It sits on a chunky little holdfast base: two stubby rounded root-feet like soft mittens splayed forward, giving it a low centre of gravity and a "planted" toy weight. Two short thick nub arms come out low on the body. From the shoulders grow FOUR broad, thick, paddle-shaped kelp blades with smoothly rounded tips — no wisps, no fringe, no thin strands — three fanned out behind the body like a rounded bouquet or a scallop shell, all fused into the body outline so the total silhouette stays one clean readable blob-with-a-fan shape. Every leaf is at least as wide as an arm, generously chunky, nothing thinner than a finger.

THE ONE IDEA: The fourth and biggest leaf is held up in both nub arms and pulled across its face like a curtain, as if to hide. But the leaf is translucent, a lighter jade veil — so its huge eyes and its glowing blush show straight through it. It is hiding, and utterly failing to hide, and that is the whole joke and the whole heart of the character.

FACE: Two enormous round black eyes with a single big white catchlight each, set wide and low — honest, hopeful, caught-in-the-act. A tiny simple mouth, a soft wobbling curve, half-shy smile. Two big soft coral-pink blush ovals on the cheeks that glow warmly through the translucent leaf, brighter where the leaf overlaps them. The face reads clearly THROUGH the veil leaf: the leaf tints it a soft green, but the eyes and blush punch through at full strength.

DETAILS (all attached, all chunky): a row of round pea-like air-bladder bumps running along the thick spine of each leaf, like buttons sewn onto the frond — big, solid, clearly connected. A pale cream oval belly patch. Two soft ridged lines on each leaf suggesting a central vein, thick enough to read at a glance.

PALETTE: deep sea-green body (mossy, saturated), lighter jade for the translucent veil leaf, bright chartreuse-lime accents on the leaf spines and bladder bumps, cream belly, coral-pink blush, dark teal outline. One bold dominant colour: green, with coral as the single warm punch.

MOOD: warm, soft, harmless, deeply huggable — a small nervous plant that wants to be your friend but needs a moment. Vinyl-toy solidity, storybook honesty, zero edginess.
